title: TDCS Modulation of Visual Cortex in Motor Imagery

funding_references.funder_name: Insitute for Cognitive Neuroscience - Higher School of Economics
Institute for Cognitive Neuroscience, Higher School of Economics

description: This dataset comprises EEG recordings from 5 participants performing motor imagery tasks under transcranial direct current stimulation (tDCS) neuromodulation targeting the visual cortex. Data were collected across 3 recording sessions using a Brain Products EEG system sampled at 5000 Hz, yielding 90 scans with a total of approximately 10.7 hours of recording. The study investigates the interaction between tDCS-induced neuromodulation and motor imagery-related brain activity, with potential applications in understanding cortical plasticity and brain-computer interfaces.
methods_description: EEG data were recorded using a Brain Products system at 5000 Hz sampling rate with 60 Hz line noise present. Participants completed motor imagery tasks across 3 recording sessions. Data were organized and processed using MNE-BIDS (version 0.6.0) in compliance with BIDS 1.7.0 standards.
